name: Restaurant-Quality Gochujang Crispy Tofu with Stir-Fried Vegetables

ingredients.ingredient: garlic, finely minced (added fresh to sauce)
fresh ginger, grated (added fresh to sauce)
red bell pepper, sliced (for stir-fried vegetables)
yellow bell pepper, sliced (for stir-fried vegetables)

instructions: Press the tofu for 30 minutes to remove excess moisture, then cut into 1-inch cubes.
Toss the tofu gently with the tofu-coating ingredients: kosher salt, cornstarch, and potato starch. Let rest for 5–10 minutes to hydrate the coating.

Heat neutral oil in a pan over medium-high heat. Fry the tofu in a single layer, turning on all sides, until deeply golden and crispy, about 12–15 minutes total. Transfer to a wire rack to maintain crispness.
Prepare the sauce base by whisking together gochujang, soy sauce, brown sugar, honey, rice vinegar, dry sherry, and water until smooth. This base can be made ahead.
Right before cooking the sauce, whisk the fresh sauce additions into the sauce base: garlic, ginger, sesame oil, and cornstarch.
In a hot pan with a small amount of oil, stir-fry the onion for 45 seconds, then add broccoli and snap peas and cook for 2–3 minutes. Add bell peppers and cook for 1–2 minutes.
Season the vegetables with soy sauce and finish with sesame oil. Remove and set aside.
Pour the prepared sauce into the pan over medium heat and cook for 45–60 seconds until thickened and glossy.
Add the crispy tofu to the sauce and toss quickly to coat evenly.
Return the vegetables to the pan and toss for no more than 30–45 seconds to combine while maintaining texture.
Serve immediately, garnished with sliced green onions and toasted sesame seeds.

storage_tips.room_temp: Serve immediately for best texture; not recommended to hold at room temperature.
storage_tips.refrigeration: Store leftovers in an airtight container for up to 3 days; reheat in a pan or air fryer to restore some crispness.
